name: Plane
what_it_does: Open-source project management platform with REST API providing full CRUD for issues, cycles, modules, pages, and analytics — a self-hostable alternative to Jira and GitHub Issues.
use_cases: Programmatically creating and updating issues from CI/CD pipelines or external tools
Syncing project state between Plane and external systems (GitHub, Slack, monitoring tools)
Generating sprint reports and analytics via the API for custom dashboards
Automating triage workflows: assigning labels, states, and members based on rules
not_for: Teams needing mature, fully-documented SDKs in multiple languages (SDK support is still maturing)
Organizations requiring enterprise-grade SLAs out of the box without self-hosting expertise
Non-technical teams who need zero-code workflow automation without API integration
best_when: Best when a development team wants full data ownership over their project management tooling, is comfortable self-hosting, and needs a REST API to integrate issue tracking into automated workflows.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need a mature, heavily-documented API ecosystem with official SDKs, or when your team lacks infrastructure capacity to maintain a self-hosted instance.

auth.notes: API key generated per user in the Plane UI under Profile > API Tokens. Passed as 'X-API-Key' header. No OAuth flow; no fine-grained scopes — key carries full user permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted (MIT license) is fully free with no feature limits. Plane Cloud free tier allows unlimited members and projects with core features.
pricing.paid_tiers: Plane Cloud Pro from $8/user/month adds advanced analytics, priority support, and extended file storage. Business and Enterprise plans available for larger teams.
pricing.notes: Self-hosted deployment via Docker Compose or Kubernetes is completely free. Community edition includes the full REST API.

performance.rate_limits: Plane Cloud enforces rate limits that vary by plan; self-hosted instances have no platform-imposed limits (limited only by your infrastructure). Specific Cloud rate limit numbers are not publicly documented.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses return JSON with an error message field but error codes are inconsistent and not always actionable. Validation errors on issue creation can be vague.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys supported. Duplicate POST requests may create duplicate issues. Agents must implement their own deduplication logic.
known_agent_gotchas: Workspace slug and project identifier must be known before any API call — no discovery endpoint returns these together
States and labels are workspace/project-specific; IDs must be fetched before use and cannot be referenced by name in the API
API documentation lags behind the UI; some endpoints available in the product are undocumented
Self-hosted instances may have different API versions than Plane Cloud — always verify against your deployment

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys carry full user-level permissions with no scoping — a compromised key exposes all user data and actions. No OAuth, no read-only tokens. Self-hosted security posture depends entirely on operator configuration.
